Keep Rallying [→ rally] X Villagers on Wood[wood], until you can effort your first and second TC.. Build a lumber camp[Lumber Camp] with one willager from the straggler trees, already collecting wood

steps.instruction: Adjust:. > Use 5 Villagers from Wood [wood] to place you first TC[Town Center] on a ressource you want to secure.. > Use 5 Villagers from Stone [stone] to place your second TC [Town Center].. > Switch Rally point [→ rally] from Home TC to food[food]

followups.instruction: > At this point you are wide open and slightly vulnerable, but don't worry to much if you lose something, since most people are on one TC. You are on 3 TC's easily effording production on all of them.. >NOTE if you are being attacked: . Wood is really important for you defending possible attacks and repairing your buildings, building towers or building a single barracks nearby, secondly food to keep your villager production running and producing spearman.. > Make sure you always kept scouting your opponents. or you have at least vision towards them (Tower or 1 scout).. Therefore you can react to possible attacks.. > Get an equal amount of Villager numbers on Food, Wood and Gold.. > In general effort at least one Barracks to defend yourself, until Castle.. > Produce some Spearman to defend yourself 6x [Spearman], until you reach castle.. > Get melee upgrades in Forge [Blacksmith] and Barracks [Barracks]. - Otherwise your units are low quality paper

phases.gameplan: - Freestyle from here. - Never stop producing VIllagers. - Build Order kind of ends here.. > Make sure your TC never stops producing villagers until you reach around 120 pop.. > At the beginning you can even overproduce to 135 Villagers, since you try to push your military numbers faster and they probably gonna die anyways during the game. - If not make sure you gonna kill them later. Otherwise you will lose fights, because you dont have higher army numbers. Which can even cost you the game.. Meanwhile,.... > ... Start to build more Millitary buildings[Barracks][Stable][Siege Workshop] to prepare an attack.. > Get your millitary numbers up at all times from now on.. > In case you feel like you have to many vills on wood, then you probably don't have enough millitary buildings or secured vision by towers on desired ressources.. > Note: you infantry can build siege.. > Your Main unit compostion should look something like this [Battering Ram],[Mangonel],[Spearman],[Ghulam],[Camel Rider].. ([Springald]against enemy Siege,[Trebuchet] against castles,[Horseman] Horseman against archery or siege.). > If you didn't received any attack until this point and never stopped producing villagers, you should be much superior in Eco, that you can easily effort a fight.. !!! Get information what is happening on the map with a few Scouts.[Scout] !!!. > Start to build a few pallisades[Palisade Wall] and towers [Outpost] for vision on ressources you aim for.. > The earlier you move out for an attack the more gold is gonna be in your enemys base to secure for yourself.. > NOTE: Without gold you probably loose.. > Be aggressive from now on. Stone can be sold for gold in the market, if you run out of gold only do it if want to keep up your momentum. But it will be cheaper for other players to purchase, which can result in more castles on the map.. > Rather use your stone to secure important areas with castles. Especially gold. don't waste it to much on stonewalls. Rather protect your base with towers. > and in FFA, every time you kill your enemy King, you get +50 towards your popluation cap. so don't forget to build houses. You want to kill 'em all.. > since your boom'd you might have the eco to invest those +50 pop cap straight in more military and go for the next kill

steps.instruction: I think you can choose between millitary wing[Military Wing] or culture wing[Culture Wing]. Since you have a lot of open upgrades at this point, i think culture Wing might boost you, getting everything in castle much easier and therefore faster[Preservation of Knowledge]. Your advancing to Imperial age becomes cheaper too

followups.instruction: Inmediatamente cuando pasas a segunda edad [Age 2] con la escuela de caballería [School of Cavalry] sacamos caballeros reales [Royal Knight] constantemente para molestar al rival y todos tus aldeanos[Villager] irán a madera [Wood]
Cuando tengas cerca de unos 5 aldeanos[villager] a la madera [wood] construyes el campamento maderero [Lumber Camp] en un bosque cercano y sigues mandando aldeanos[Villager] a madera[Wood]
Cuando hayas sacado tú tercer Caballero Real [Royal Knight] investigas Hacha de doble filo [Double Broadax]en tu campamento maderero [Lumber Camp]
Con la madera [wood] que te vaya sobrando, construyes una Galería de Tiro con Arco [Archery Range] y una herrería [Blacksmith] y comienzas a sacar Arqueros [Archer]

En este punto, Juana [Jeanne d'Arc - Peasant] ya tendrá experiencia suficiente para subir de nivel, dependiendo de la partida puede elegir Juana Campeona [Jeanne Darc Woman at Arms] o Juana Arquera [Jeanne Darc Hunter], sea cual sea el caso, vas con Juana a por el Jabalí [boar] junto con 8 aldeanos de la comida [Food] y ellos construyen un molino [Mill] cerca del Jabalí [Boar] y mejoras la tecnología de técnicas de supervivencia [Survival Techniques]
Mandas 2 aldeanos [Villager] más al oro [Gold] y desde este punto es Adaptación según la partida

[Tughluqabad Fort] governors priority: [Bhakkar] (tier 3) followed by [Uch] (tier 1). Then pick based on the situation:. [Ajmer] if under inmediate threat or to help secure an exposed [Gold] vein.. [Sehwan] if you manage to grab some [relic].. [Jalor] for a stronger cavalry army. Preferably leave it for later.. [Multan] if you don't need/want the others. A bit unnecessary

steps.instruction: [Hisar Academy]: Can generate up to 300 [Food] and 300 [Gold] per minute, which is incredibly useful for the ideal [Tuglhaq Dynasty] army that requires a lot of [Gold].. [Palace of the Sultan]: The only tier 4 governor effects that are worth are [Bhakkar] for more resources and [Jalor] for a bit of extra health on cavalry or [Sehwan] for more healing on [Healer Elephant].. Since we have already built and upgraded [Bhakkar], we can discard that option.. [Jalor] only grants 15 more health compared to the tier 3 effect, so it's not really worth it, although it can be useful for a timing attack right after aging up; since we are skipping the several minutes it takes to build and upgrade the [Tughluqabad Fort] (and the [Stone] cost).. [Sehwan] is ok, but nothing too crazy, and adding [Healer Elephant] is very expensive for the stats they provide. It's good to have a few of them, but investing an entire landmark to buff them is not optimal
